FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Q1: Do I need to manually adjust Lumina, or does it really do everything automatically?

A: Lumina is designed to be as automatic as possible. Once you connect your calendar and set your basic preferences (work start time, preference for warm vs. cool during focus hours, etc.), it handles everything. The ambient light sensor continuously reads your room, the circadian algorithm adjusts based on time of day, and the calendar integration ensures you're lit perfectly for your schedule. Most users set it once and never touch it again.

That said, you have full manual control if you ever want it. One-touch brightness, easy color temperature adjustment—all at your fingertips. But you won't need it often.

---

Q2: How does Lumina know what color temperature is "right"?

A: Lumina uses two primary inputs: circadian science and your personal preferences.

From a science perspective, your body naturally responds to light cues. Cool, bright light (around 5000-6500K) promotes alertness and focus—that's what you want during your core work hours. Warm light (around 2700-3000K) signals your body that it's time to rest—that's what you want in the evening.

Lumina starts with this science-backed default, but it also learns. If you manually adjust your light preference at certain times, Lumina remembers. Over time, it gets smarter about exactly what you like.

The calendar integration adds another layer: if you have a meeting scheduled, Lumina shifts slightly cooler to ensure you look sharp and alert on camera.

---

Q3: Does Lumina work with Google Calendar? What about Outlook or Apple Calendar?

A: Lumina currently integrates with Google Calendar and Outlook. If you use Apple Calendar, you can sync it to Google Calendar (most email providers do this automatically), and Lumina will read it that way.

We're working on direct Apple Calendar integration and will have it ready in Q2 2024.

---

Q4: What if my office doesn't get natural light (or gets way too much)? Will Lumina still work?

A: Yes, actually Lumina works even better in these situations.

If your office has no natural light, Lumina becomes your primary light source and manages your circadian rhythm entirely. You'll feel the difference in focus and energy.

If your office gets blasted with harsh sunlight all afternoon, Lumina's ambient light sensor detects this and automatically dims itself to maintain comfortable brightness. It's like having a personal dimmer that's always working on your behalf.

In both cases, you get perfectly balanced light without ever thinking about it.

---

Q5: How long is the setup process? Do I need an electrician or IT person?

A: Setup takes about 5 minutes and requires zero technical expertise.

1. Unbox Lumina and place it on your desk
2. Plug the included power cable into a standard outlet (no special wiring needed)
3. Download the Lumina app on your phone or go to our web portal
4. Connect to your WiFi (standard setup, same as connecting any smart home device)
5. Connect your Google Calendar or Outlook
6. Set your preferred work hours and light preferences (optional—defaults are already optimized)

That's it. You're done. Start working.

If you have any trouble, our support team is available 24/7 via chat, email, or phone.

---

Q6: Is Lumina compatible with my existing smart home setup (Alexa, Google Home, Apple HomeKit)?

A: Yes. Lumina works with all three.

You can control Lumina via voice commands ("Alexa, brighten my Lumina," "Hey Siri, warm up my light") or integrate it into routines. For example, you could create a morning routine that brews coffee and sets your Lumina to energizing cool light, or an evening routine that dims everything as part of your wind-down.

That said, most users find they don't need to do this—Lumina is smart enough to handle it on its own. But the integrations are there if you want them.

---

Q7: What's the warranty and return policy?

A: Lumina comes with a 2-year manufacturer's warranty covering all defects in materials and workmanship.

If you're not completely happy, we offer a 30-day risk-free trial. Order, use it for a month, and if you don't love it, send it back for a full refund. No questions asked. No restocking fees.

---

Q8: How much does it actually cost? Is $149 the final price?

A: $149 is our launch price and final price. That includes the lamp, smart control system, all software, and free shipping.

We don't have subscription fees or hidden costs. You pay $149 once, and Lumina is yours forever.

---

Q9: Does Lumina save on electricity costs compared to a regular desk lamp?

A: Yes, though probably not as much as you'd think.

Lumina uses LED technology, which is already about 80% more efficient than traditional incandescent bulbs. Compared to other LED lamps, Lumina saves an additional 15-20% because the ambient light sensor prevents over-lighting (you're not paying to light more than you need).

Over a year, most users save about $8-12 in electricity costs compared to a standard desk lamp. The real savings come from improved productivity and focus—that's where the ROI is massive.

---

Q10: How often do I need to replace bulbs or do maintenance?

A: Never.

Lumina uses integrated LED modules rated for 50,000 hours of use. That's about 13 years of continuous, 24/7 operation. In real-world use (8 hours a day), you're looking at 17 years before you'd even think about replacement.

There's no maintenance required. No bulbs to swap. No filters. Just occasional light dusting of the exterior. Lumina is designed to last.

A: Absolutely.

Lumina's entire value proposition is about matching light to your actual work schedule. When you set up your calendar and work hours, tell Lumina your real schedule—whether that's 10pm to 6am or 5am to 1pm.

Lumina will adjust accordingly, supporting your circadian rhythm and alertness around your actual work, not around "normal" business hours.

This is actually where Lumina shines (pun intended) because it doesn't assume anything about your schedule.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
THE SCIENCE BEHIND LUMINA
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Lumina is built on three core scientific principles:

1. CIRCADIAN RHYTHM ALIGNMENT
Your body operates on a ~24-hour cycle regulated by light exposure. Cool, bright light tells your brain it's daytime → time to be alert and productive. Warm, dim light signals nighttime → time to rest and recover.

Most people working indoors ignore this entirely, bathing themselves in consistent artificial light (or harsh blue light) all day. Your circadian rhythm gets confused. You feel tired in the afternoon and wired at night.

Lumina restores the natural light cycle, boosting alertness when you need it and supporting sleep when it matters.

Research: Exposure to bright light during the day increases alertness and productivity by up to 25%. Proper evening light improves sleep onset by 30%.

2. COLOR TEMPERATURE AND FOCUS
Different light colors (color temperature, measured in Kelvin) affect cognitive function differently.

Cool light (5000-6500K): Increases alertness, focus, and attention span. Optimal for analytical work, meetings, and creative problem-solving.

Warm light (2700-3000K): Promotes relaxation and creativity. Optimal for wind-down, learning, and tasks that benefit from a calm state.

Blue light (6000K+) at night: Suppresses melatonin production, disrupting sleep.

Lumina shifts through the optimal range throughout your day, supporting whatever you're doing.

Research: Studies show cool light improves focus by 18% and warm light improves sleep quality by 27%.

3. AMBIENT LIGHT ADAPTATION
Your eyes continuously adapt to the brightness around you (a process called adaptation). Sitting in a dark room makes you think a medium-bright light is very bright. Sitting in a bright room makes that same light seem dim.

A fixed-brightness lamp is always fighting against your room's changing light. You end up with either too much light (eye strain, glare) or too little (squinting, fatigue).

Lumina's sensor continuously reads your room's brightness and adjusts to maintain perfect balance—neither too bright nor too dim. This reduces eye strain by up to 42%.

Research: Improper lighting balance increases eye strain, reduces focus, and contributes to digital eyestrain syndrome in 65% of office workers.

Q1: Do I need to manually adjust Lumina, or does it really do everything automatically?

Q2: How does Lumina know what color temperature is "right"?

A: Lumina uses two primary inputs: circadian science and your personal preferences.

From a science perspective, your body naturally responds to light cues. Cool, bright light (around 5000-6500K) promotes alertness and focus—that's what you want during your core work hours. Warm light (around 2700-3000K) signals your body that it's time to rest—that's what you want in the evening.

Lumina starts with this science-backed default, but it also learns. If you manually adjust your light preference at certain times, Lumina remembers. Over time, it gets smarter about exactly what you like.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
THE SCIENCE BEHIND LUMINA
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Lumina is built on three core scientific principles:

1. CIRCADIAN RHYTHM ALIGNMENT
Your body operates on a ~24-hour cycle regulated by light exposure. Cool, bright light tells your brain it's daytime → time to be alert and productive. Warm, dim light signals nighttime → time to rest and recover.

Most people working indoors ignore this entirely, bathing themselves in consistent artificial light (or harsh blue light) all day. Your circadian rhythm gets confused. You feel tired in the afternoon and wired at night.

Lumina restores the natural light cycle, boosting alertness when you need it and supporting sleep when it matters.

Research: Exposure to bright light during the day increases alertness and productivity by up to 25%. Proper evening light improves sleep onset by 30%.

2. COLOR TEMPERATURE AND FOCUS
Different light colors (color temperature, measured in Kelvin) affect cognitive function differently.

Cool light (5000-6500K): Increases alertness, focus, and attention span. Optimal for analytical work, meetings, and creative problem-solving.

Warm light (2700-3000K): Promotes relaxation and creativity. Optimal for wind-down, learning, and tasks that benefit from a calm state.

Blue light (6000K+) at night: Suppresses melatonin production, disrupting sleep.

Lumina shifts through the optimal range throughout your day, supporting whatever you're doing.

Research: Studies show cool light improves focus by 18% and warm light improves sleep quality by 27%.

3. AMBIENT LIGHT ADAPTATION
Your eyes continuously adapt to the brightness around you (a process called adaptation). Sitting in a dark room makes you think a medium-bright light is very bright. Sitting in a bright room makes that same light seem dim.

A fixed-brightness lamp is always fighting against your room's changing light. You end up with either too much light (eye strain, glare) or too little (squinting, fatigue).

Lumina's sensor continuously reads your room's brightness and adjusts to maintain perfect balance—neither too bright nor too dim. This reduces eye strain by up to 42%.

Research: Improper lighting balance increases eye strain, reduces focus, and contributes to digital eyestrain syndrome in 65% of office workers.

After noticing the pattern—energy crashes at 3pm, inconsistent sleep, eye strain by the end of the workday—they started researching. What they found was startling: lighting is the #1 overlooked factor in remote work productivity and wellness.

Despite billions spent on ergonomic chairs, standing desks, and productivity software, almost nobody was paying attention to light. Yet light directly affects circadian rhythm, focus, eye health, and sleep quality.

They spent 18 months researching circadian science, testing dozens of prototypes, and working with 500+ remote workers to validate what works.
